title: bursera subpubescens
intro: Bursera subpubescens, also known as the "Sapotillo" or "Sapotilla", is a plant species in the family Burseraceae. It is native to Central and South America and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties. The plant's leaves and roots are rich in antioxidants and have been used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, fever, and skin conditions, providing benefits like reduced inflammation, improved digestion, and boosted immune system. Bursera subpubescens can be prepared as an herbal infusion, tea, or tincture, and is also used in traditional folk medicine as a treatment for various ailments.
